RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to him. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claim that defendant owed him a duty. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Desiree Winn of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13645 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
